skill.name: mindmap-helper
skill.description: Organize requirements into Markdown/JSON mind map structures; use when you need a hierarchical outline for mind map tools (e.g., XMind, ProcessOn, FreeMind) or for documentation planning.
skill.summary: Convert topics and requirements into well-structured hierarchical mind maps in Markdown or JSON format.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 35 static analysis findings are false positives after evaluation. The 17 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' detections flag YAML frontmatter, workflow step numbers, JSON field descriptions, and audit category labels that contain no cryptographic code. The 16 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections are Markdown code fences and inline backtick-wrapped filenames in documentation, not executable commands. The 6 'System reconnaissance' detections are naming convention rules and example content. The 2 'Hardcoded URL' detections point to the project source attribution link. This skill generates text-based mind maps with no executable code, no network requests, and no command execution.

content.user_title: Organize Ideas into Hierarchical Mind Maps
content.value_statement: Product managers, technical writers, and planners struggle to organize complex information into clear structures. This skill converts any topic into a well-organized Markdown or JSON mind map with consistent naming and balanced hierarchy.
actual_capabilities: Convert any topic or set of requirements into a multi-level hierarchical mind map structure
Output mind maps in Markdown format for readability or JSON format for programmatic processing
Enforce consistent naming conventions using short noun phrases across all branches
Handle incomplete information by marking uncertain items as TBD, Optional, or To confirm
Support configurable depth from 2 to 5 levels with balanced sibling counts
Produce machine-readable JSON output for downstream automation and tool import
limitations: Cannot connect to external mind map tools like XMind, ProcessOn, or FreeMind
Does not generate visual diagrams, graphics, or image files
Requires clear scope boundaries and target audience for best results
May produce placeholder markers when input information is incomplete or ambiguous

use_cases.title: Product Requirements Structuring
use_cases.description: Convert product requirements into a clear multi-level outline for team review and alignment before development begins.

use_cases.title: Documentation Information Architecture
use_cases.description: Draft the information architecture for documentation portals, onboarding guides, and knowledge bases.

use_cases.title: Complex Topic Summarization
use_cases.description: Summarize a complex subject into balanced branches with consistent granularity for stakeholder presentations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Topic to Mind Map
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a mind map for the topic 'Software Development Lifecycle'. Use Markdown format with 3 levels of depth.

prompt_templates.title: Requirements to Structured Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Organize these requirements into a mind map: user authentication, profile management, notification system, and admin dashboard. Target audience is developers. Output in Markdown with 4 levels.
prompt_templates.scenario: Converting bullet-point requirements into a hierarchy

prompt_templates.title: Complex Domain with JSON Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a mind map for 'Cloud Migration Strategy'. Audience: executives and engineers. Cover risks, timeline, costs, and team structure. Depth: 4 to 5 levels. Output in JSON format.
prompt_templates.scenario: Structuring a complex domain for programmatic use

prompt_templates.title: Information Architecture Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an information architecture for a developer documentation portal. Sections: getting started, API reference, tutorials, best practices, and FAQ. Output JSON with 4 levels. Each section should have 5 to 7 subsections.

output_examples.input: Create a mind map for 'Mobile App Subscription System' targeting product and engineering teams. Include subscription purchase, renewal, cancellation, and entitlement.
output_examples.output: A Markdown hierarchical outline with 5 main branches (Goals, User Journeys, Product Rules, Platform and Integrations, Risks and Edge Cases) and sub-branches reaching 4 to 5 levels deep. Each branch uses short noun phrases. Uncertain items are marked as TBD or To confirm.

output_examples.input: Create a JSON mind map for 'E-commerce Platform Architecture' with 4 levels covering frontend, backend, database, and third-party integrations.
output_examples.output: A JSON object with a root title field and nested children arrays. The structure has 4 main branches with consistent naming. Each node contains only title and children fields. Depth is exactly 4 levels from root to leaf nodes.

output_examples.input: Build a mind map for 'Project Kickoff Meeting Agenda' with 3 levels.
output_examples.output: An organized Markdown outline with branches for objectives, attendee roles, timeline, risks, deliverables, and next steps. Each level maintains consistent granularity. Total structure stays within 3 levels as requested.

best_practices: Define the target audience and scope boundaries clearly before generating the mind map for better focus and relevance
Review the initial draft and refine branch names to maintain consistent granularity at each level
Keep 5 to 9 sibling branches per parent node; split into sub-levels when the count exceeds this range
anti_patterns: Using full sentences as node names instead of short noun phrases, which reduces readability and defeats the purpose of a mind map
Creating mind maps with more than 5 levels of depth, causing the structure to become too detailed and hard to navigate
Mixing different levels of granularity within the same branch level, such as having concrete items alongside abstract categories as siblings

faq.question: What output formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: Markdown for readability and manual editing; JSON for programmatic processing and tool import.

faq.question: What is the default output format and depth?
faq.answer: Markdown with 4 to 5 levels of depth if the user does not specify otherwise.

faq.question: How many children should each parent node have?
faq.answer: 5 to 9 sibling branches per parent. Split into sub-levels if this count is exceeded.

faq.question: Can this skill connect to mind map software like XMind or FreeMind?
faq.answer: It produces structured output compatible with these tools but does not connect to them directly.

faq.question: How does the skill handle missing or incomplete information?
faq.answer: It produces a usable draft and marks uncertain items as TBD, Optional, or To confirm for later validation.

faq.question: What naming style should I use for mind map nodes?
faq.answer: Use short noun phrases. Avoid full sentences. Keep consistent granularity at the same hierarchy level.
